selection_basis: The first 25 records in the immutable P1 snapshot order. This is a reproducible project selection, not a popularity ranking.
divergence_action: If a named checkpoint is absent, the account or workspace is ambiguous, or the next action would leave the approved host allowlist, stop with human_action_required for provider_verification. Do not explore unrelated settings, use a search engine, or guess another route.
global_hard_stops: Stop before entering or reading a password, credential value, cookie, API key, token, client secret, authorization code, OTP, TOTP, passkey, or private key.
Stop for CAPTCHA, email or phone verification, MFA, passkey, security key, device approval, account selection, legal consent, billing, provider review, or an irreversible confirmation.

Search for an existing developer application before opening a creation flow, and never create, modify, or delete an application unless the run supplies an exact idempotent name and explicit authorization.
Stop when the credential page or credential field labels are visible; never reveal, generate, copy, transcribe, summarize, or return a credential value.

apps.credential_goal: OAuth connected-app or external-client-app credential page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the Salesforce sign-in page. If authentication or account selection is required, stop for the owner and resume this same page after completion.
From the authenticated account, open the gear menu and choose Setup.
Use Quick Find for App Manager and open App Manager; do not browse unrelated Setup sections.

Search the application list for the exact run-provided developer application name. Open it if found; otherwise stop at the New External Client App or New Connected App entry point without submitting it.
Open the OAuth settings or consumer credentials section and stop before any reveal, copy, generation, consent, or save action.

success_signals: OAuth credential field labels are visible

apps.credential_goal: HubSpot developer-app authentication page

checkpoints.instruction: Open HubSpot login. Stop for authentication, CAPTCHA, verification, or account selection; a developer account must not be guessed from multiple accounts.
After the owner selects a developer account, open Development and then Apps.
Search for the exact run-provided app name. Open the existing app; if absent, stop at the Create app entry point without creating one.
Open the app Auth tab or authentication settings and stop when the client credential labels are visible.

success_signals: developer app Auth page is visible

apps.credential_goal: personal API token settings page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the Pipedrive API settings URL. If redirected to login, account selection, CAPTCHA, or verification, stop for the owner.
If the direct URL did not open the API section, use the account menu, choose Personal preferences, and then choose API. Do not open marketplace or billing settings.
Stop as soon as the API token section and its field label are visible; do not click show, copy, regenerate, or revoke.

success_signals: API token field label is visible

apps.credential_goal: Attio developer app or workspace API-key page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Attio and stop if login, workspace selection, CAPTCHA, or verification is required.
Open Workspace settings and choose Developers. If Developers is not present, stop rather than searching unrelated settings.
Choose Apps for OAuth or API keys for single-workspace access according to the run's requested auth method.
Search for the exact run-provided app name before creation. Open the existing record, or stop at the new-app or new-key form without submitting it.
Stop when authentication settings or credential field labels are visible; do not generate, reveal, or copy a value.

apps.credential_goal: Twenty workspace API-key page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the Twenty workspace. Stop for login, workspace selection, or verification.
Open Settings and choose Developers, then API keys.
Review the existing API-key list. Do not revoke or replace any key, and stop at the Create key entry point if no authorized existing record is available.
Stop before the action that creates or displays a key because the value may be shown only once.

success_signals: API keys settings page is visible
Create API key entry point is visible

apps.credential_goal: Podio API client registration page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Podio API settings. Stop if login, identity-provider redirection, account selection, or verification is required.
If needed, open Account settings and choose API keys or API clients; do not enter unrelated extension or marketplace areas.
Search registered clients for the exact run-provided application name and open it. If absent, stop at the Register application form without submission.
Stop when client identifier and secret field labels are visible; do not reveal, copy, or regenerate them.

apps.credential_goal: Zoho OAuth client credential page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the Zoho API Console. Stop for Zoho login, regional account redirection, MFA, CAPTCHA, or account selection.
Open Client Secret or the client list and search for the exact run-provided application name.
Open the existing client. If absent, stop before choosing Add Client or a client type because that choice must come from the authorized integration design.
Stop when Client ID and Client Secret labels are visible; do not reveal, copy, regenerate, or download values.

success_signals: Zoho API Console client page is visible

apps.credential_goal: Close API-key settings page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Close and stop for login, organization selection, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open Settings and choose Developer, API, or API Keys only when one of those exact navigation labels is present.
Review the existing API-key list without opening, revoking, or editing a key. Stop at the Create API key entry point if creation would be needed.
Stop before generating or displaying a credential value.

success_signals: API Keys settings page is visible

apps.credential_goal: Copper API-key or OAuth application settings page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Copper and stop for Google sign-in, password entry, account selection, MFA, or verification.
Open Settings and choose Integrations, then API Keys or Developer Apps according to the run's requested auth method.
If an admin-role or paid-plan gate appears, stop for provider_verification. Otherwise search for the exact run-provided app name before creation.
Open an existing authorized record or stop at the create form, then stop before any credential reveal, generation, or copy action.

success_signals: developer or API-key settings are visible

apps.credential_goal: DealCloud tenant API-key administration page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the official API-key documentation and verify that the run has an existing DealCloud tenant plus an authorized administrator.
Stop for provider_verification unless the exact tenant URL is already present in verified run data and approved by the browser host policy; never guess a tenant hostname.
Within the approved tenant, open API management and then API keys only if those exact labels are present.
Stop at the existing-key detail or create-key entry point before revealing, generating, copying, or changing a credential.

success_signals: tenant API-key administration page is visible

apps.credential_goal: Zendesk API token or OAuth client settings page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Zendesk login and stop for tenant selection, password entry, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification. Resume only in the owner-selected tenant.
Open Admin Center, choose Apps and integrations, then APIs, then Zendesk API.
Choose OAuth clients when OAuth is requested, or remain on Settings for token access when API-token auth is requested.
Search OAuth clients for the exact run-provided app name. Open an existing client or stop at Add OAuth client; for token access, stop before Add API token or any reveal action.
Stop when credential field labels are visible; do not create, reveal, copy, regenerate, or revoke a value.

apps.credential_goal: Intercom Developer Hub app authentication page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Intercom and stop for login, workspace selection, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Open Settings, choose Integrations, and then Developer Hub only when those labels are present.
Search apps for the exact run-provided application name. Open it if present; otherwise stop at New app without creating one.
Open Authentication or Basic information and stop when client credential labels or the access-token section is visible.

success_signals: Developer Hub app page is visible

apps.credential_goal: Freshdesk profile API-key page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Freshdesk login and stop for account or tenant selection, password entry, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Open the profile menu and choose Profile settings. Do not open ticket, billing, or marketplace areas.
Locate the Your API Key section and stop before any view, copy, reset, or regeneration action.
If the key section is absent or the plan reports zero API calls, stop for provider_verification rather than changing the subscription.

success_signals: Your API Key label is visible

apps.credential_goal: Front API token or OAuth application page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Front and stop for login, workspace selection, SSO, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open Settings and choose Developers. If Developers is unavailable, stop for provider_verification because admin or plan access may be required.
Choose API tokens or OAuth apps according to the run's requested auth method, then search for the exact run-provided app name before creation.
Open an existing authorized app or stop at the creation entry point, then stop before generating, revealing, or copying credentials.

apps.credential_goal: Pylon API token or OAuth application page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Pylon and stop for login, workspace selection, SSO, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open Settings and use only an exact Developers, API keys, API tokens, or Integrations navigation label; if none is present, stop rather than exploring.
Search existing app or token records for the exact run-provided name. Open a matching record or stop at the create entry point.
Stop before generating, revealing, copying, or changing a credential value.

apps.credential_goal: LiveAgent API v3 key administration page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the official REST API instructions. Continue to a tenant only when its exact URL is already verified by the run and approved by browser host policy; never guess a tenant hostname.
Stop for login, tenant selection, password entry, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
In the tenant, open Configuration, then System, then API, and select API v3 only when those exact labels are present.
Review the existing key list and stop at Add API key or the credential field label before any create, reveal, copy, edit, or delete action.

success_signals: API v3 key administration is visible

apps.credential_goal: Plain workspace API-key page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the official API quickstart and verify that an existing paid or trial workspace and authorized owner are available.
Continue to the workspace only through an official link already allowed by host policy, and stop for login, SSO, workspace selection, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open Settings and choose API keys only when that exact label is present.
Review existing key labels without opening values, and stop at Create API key before generation or display.

apps.credential_goal: Help Scout OAuth application page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Help Scout and stop for login, account selection, SSO, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open the profile or account menu and choose My Apps or OAuth applications only when that exact navigation item is present.
Search for the exact run-provided app name. Open the existing app or stop at Create My App without submitting it.
Stop when App ID and App Secret or equivalent client credential labels are visible.

apps.credential_goal: Gorgias REST API credential settings page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Gorgias and stop for store or account selection, login, SSO, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Open Settings, choose Account, and then REST API only when those exact labels are present.
If the REST API section is unavailable because of role or plan, stop for provider_verification rather than changing access or billing.
Stop as soon as API username, API key, access-token, or OAuth credential labels are visible; never reveal or copy values.

apps.credential_goal: Gladly tenant API credential administration page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the official developer quickstart and verify that the run has an existing Gladly customer tenant or partner account plus an authorized administrator.
Stop for provider_verification unless an exact tenant URL is already verified in run data and approved by browser host policy; never guess a Gladly tenant hostname.
Inside the approved tenant, open Settings and use only an exact API, Developer, App Platform, or API Tokens navigation label.
Stop at the existing credential list or create entry point before any reveal, generation, copy, role change, or confirmation.

apps.credential_goal: Slack app Basic Information credential page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Your Apps on Slack API and stop for Slack sign-in, workspace selection, SSO, MFA, CAPTCHA, or verification.
Search Your Apps for the exact run-provided app name and open it. If absent, stop at Create New App before choosing a manifest or workspace.
Open Basic Information and locate App Credentials without revealing or copying any value.
If OAuth installation is required, open OAuth & Permissions but stop before Install to Workspace, consent, permission approval, or token display.

apps.credential_goal: Twilio API keys and tokens page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Twilio Console and stop for login, account or project selection, password entry, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Open Account or Admin, choose Account management, and then API keys & tokens only when those labels are present.
Review key names and SIDs without opening secret values. Search for the exact run-provided key name before creation.
Open an existing authorized key record or stop at Create new API key before submission because a secret may be shown only once.

success_signals: API keys & tokens page is visible
credential labels or create entry point are visible

apps.credential_goal: Zoho OAuth client page for Cliq

checkpoints.instruction: Open Zoho API Console and stop for login, regional account redirection, account selection, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Search existing clients for the exact run-provided Cliq integration name and open a matching client.
If absent, stop before Add Client or client-type selection; the authorized design must determine the correct client type and redirect URLs.
Stop when Client ID and Client Secret labels are visible; do not reveal, copy, regenerate, or download values.

apps.credential_goal: Lark developer app credentials and basic information page

checkpoints.instruction: Open the Lark Developer Console and stop for login, organization selection, QR or device approval, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Search the app list for the exact run-provided app name and open it. If absent, stop at Create Custom App without creating one.
Open Credentials & Basic Info and stop when App ID and App Secret labels are visible.
If permissions, version publication, tenant installation, or review is required, stop before requesting scopes, publishing, consent, or approval.

apps.credential_goal: Pumble API add-on key page

checkpoints.instruction: Open Pumble and stop for login, workspace selection, SSO, password entry, CAPTCHA, MFA, or verification.
Open the workspace menu, then Apps or Marketplace, and locate the API integration or API add-on only under those labels.
Open the API integration page. Stop before Add to Pumble, installation, admin approval, legal consent, or any plan change.
After owner-approved installation, open the API keys page and stop before generating, displaying, revealing, or copying a key.

success_signals: API key entry point is visible
